# Figure description: 44.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/M-42-II/44.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **predictive/prognostic geological map** ("Карта прогнозов") with elements showing regularities in the distribution of mineral deposits. It is a thematic interpretation map compiled at 1:200,000 scale, overlaying geological structure with mineral occurrence data and prospectivity zonation. It belongs to the category of **metallogenic/prognostic maps** common in Soviet-era exploration methodology.

---

## Coverage and Subject

- **Map sheet:** M-42-II (номенклатурный лист М-42-ІІ)
- **Scale:** 1:200,000
- **Year:** 1984
- **Drawing number:** Чертёж №39 (Drawing No. 39)
- **Organization:** Министерство геологии Казахской ССР, Центрально-Казахстанское производственное геологическое объединение (Ministry of Geology of the Kazakh SSR, Central Kazakhstan Production Geological Association)
- **Archive stamp:** Респ. Казахстанский геологический фонд, Инв. № 36572 — confirming this belongs to Report 36572
- **Compiler:** Науменко В.В. (Naumenko V.V.), compiled at the Central Geological-Survey Expedition
- **Geographic coverage:** A portion of northern/central Kazakhstan corresponding to sheet M-42-II, showing a region with Precambrian to Cenozoic geological units

---

## Key Content

### Geological Units (mapped formations, labeled with standard Soviet stratigraphic notation):

- **KZ** — Кайнозой (Cenozoic): widely distributed as irregular patches across the map, particularly in the northern, western, and southeastern areas, representing sedimentary cover
- **R₁Ar** (Riphean-Archean) — older crystalline basement exposures
- **R₂Ar, R₃Ar** — Riphean subdivisions of Archean-Proterozoic metamorphic complexes
- **R₁atm, R₂atm, R₃atm** — Riphean formations, likely metamorphic-volcanic sequences (the "atm" suffix possibly denoting a specific local stratigraphic unit)
- **R₁gr, R₂gr** — Riphean granitoid intrusions
- **Р₁D, Р₂D** — possibly Proterozoic-Devonian units
- **C₁, C₁tr** — Lower Carboniferous (possibly trachytic or transitional facies)
- **D₁** — Devonian
- **O₁** — Ordovician
- **Г** — Гранит/Гранитоиды (Granite/Granitoids), labeled in several large areas across the western and eastern parts of the map

### Structural Features:

- **Contour lines** (isolines) in the eastern portion of the map with values labeled: **1000, 1200, 1400, 1600, 1800, 2000** — these likely represent **depth-to-basement contours** (in meters) or structural elevation contours, showing a deepening basin toward the east-southeast
- **Dashed curvilinear lines** — represent interpreted structural boundaries or tectonic zones (fault systems, anticlinal/synclinal axes)
- A prominent **arcuate/curved structural zone** running from the northwest to southeast through the center of the map, marked by red hatching — this appears to be a **prospective metallogenic belt or tectonic suture zone**

### Mineralization and Prospectivity Symbols:

- **Red diagonal hatching zones** — two prominent zones of red hatching running diagonally across the map (NW–SE orientation): these likely denote **prognostic/prospective areas** for specific mineral types (commonly copper, gold, or polymetals in such Soviet maps)
- **Green outlined elongate shapes** — clustered predominantly in the central-western portion of the map; these likely represent **known or predicted ore occurrences**, possibly copper (Cu) deposits or prospects given the "Cu" labels visible nearby
- **Dot symbols (filled circles)** — scattered across the map, labeled with "Cu" — representing **known copper occurrences or deposits**
- **Small green ellipses/lenses** — may represent skarn or contact-metasomatic mineralization zones
- **Red dotted line** — a curved boundary running through the central map area, possibly delineating a **prognostic ore zone boundary** or a geophysical anomaly contour

### Mineral Labels Visible:
- **Cu** — Copper occurrences, labeled at multiple points
- **Ru** — possibly Rutile or another mineral (one point in the eastern area)
- Possibly **CuAr, CuFr** — copper-bearing associations
